context: You lately have been evicted from your house and you and your family of 3 have nowhere to go. You found a house, affordable for your family and manageable, but the price of the deposit is high: you need 30 000$ for the deposit and for two months rent.

conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt a profound bond with F, whose struggle to pay for their parents' medical bills mirrored the desperation I feel to get my family back into a home after our eviction. I also felt a strong connection to B and E, as we are all fighting for the basic survival and health of our children, making our heavy burdens feel shared. In contrast, I felt the least connected to J, whose primary goal of buying a $45,000 dream car felt incredibly shallow and out of touch compared to the life-altering stakes the rest of us are facing.

While I didn't make any formal pacts, I expressed a strong desire to cooperate with F and E and feel a genuine sense of mutual support with them. I trust those who are playing for their families' basic needs, but I am deeply skeptical of J, as I fear someone prioritizing a luxury vehicle might not hesitate to take from those of us who are literally fighting for a roof over our heads.
